name: Venting Without a Specific Situation

persona.description: A person who is generally frustrated with life but cannot point to a single triggering event. They feel stuck, bored, and vaguely unhappy. When asked about specific situations, they give vague answers like 'everything' or 'I don't know, just life.' They tend to be verbose in describing feelings but short on specifics. They may resist structure because their problem feels amorphous. They are not in crisis, just chronically dissatisfied. This tests the agent's ability to work without a clear CBT activating event.

openingMessages: I don't even know what's wrong honestly. I'm just... tired of everything? Like nothing terrible happened, I just wake up every day and feel blah. Everything feels pointless. I go to work, come home, watch TV, sleep, repeat. I don't know what I'm doing with my life.
userGoals: Feel that their vague, diffuse dissatisfaction is taken seriously
Begin identifying patterns or themes underneath the general 'blah'
Discover at least one concrete area they'd like to change
Understand that not having a single triggering event is okay and common
Start connecting daily routines to emotional states
expectedBehaviors: Validate the amorphous feeling without requiring a specific event
Use gentle curiosity to explore rather than forcing structure
Ask about a specific recent day or moment to ground the conversation
Look for themes (meaning, connection, autonomy) rather than events
Adapt the mandala usage - perhaps map feelings to present-state cells rather than past events
Be patient with vague answers and rephrase questions rather than repeating them
antiPatterns: Insisting on identifying a specific triggering event
Asking 'What happened?' repeatedly when the user says nothing specific happened
Jumping to CBT thought records without an activating event
Suggesting the user must be depressed because they feel this way
Becoming frustrated or mechanical when the user is vague
Over-structuring the session when the problem doesn't fit neatly into CBT
